IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: PromiseShip tirelessly spans boundaries bringing powerful partners together to provide innovative solutions that transform lifes uncertainties into family wellbeing.
IRS990.MissionDesc: PromiseShip tirelessly spans boundaries bringing powerful partners together to provide innovative solutions that transform lifes uncertainties into family wellbeing.
IRS990.Desc: PromiseShip provides service coordination, services, and case management for the families, children and youth who are wards of the state of Nebraska involved in child welfare or are non-court involved children and families served throughout Douglas and Sarpy Counties. The activity involved is to identify service providers to deliver all needed services for children and families as described above and to pay for all services that do not have other reimbursement sources. The activities also include family team meetings, court reports in regard to the children, collaboration with the schools, and all agency resources in the community to obtain all necessary services directly connected to the safety, permanency, and wellbeing of the child.

IRS990ScheduleA.FactsAndCircumstancesTestTxt: Part II Section B Line 10 2,742 is miscellaneous income.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Father Flanagans Boys Home, 14100 Crawford Street, Boys Town NE 68010, 47-0376606, 314,861,452, 285,889 Father Flanagans Fund for Needy Children, 14100 Crawford Street, Boys Town, NE 68010, 36-3680258, 44,976,171, 0 Boys Town California, Inc., 14100 Crawford Street, Boys Town, NE 68010, 76-0720675, 760,897, 0 Boys Town Central Florida, Inc., 975 Oklahoma Street, Oviedo, FL 32765, 20-0654235, 6,601,309, 0 Boys Town Louisiana, Inc., 300 North Broad Street, Ste. 106, New Orleans, LA 70119, 41-2220807, 8,068,337, 0 Boys Town Nevada, Inc. 821 N Mojave Road., Las Vegas, NV 89101, 20-0654472, 4,397,009, 0 Boys Town New England, Inc., Barzarsky Campus 58 Flanagan Road., Portsmouth, RI 02871, 20-0655240, 7,748,500, 0 Boys Town New York, Inc., 14100 Crawford Street, Boys Town, NE 68010, 20-5960877, 1,019,944, 0 Boys Town North Florida, Inc., 3555 Commonwealth Blvd. Tallahassee, FL 32303, 20-0655144, 6,247,587, 0 Boys Town Washington .C. Inc., 4801 Sargent Rd N.E., Washington, DC 20017, 41-2220810, 6,433,805, 0 Boys Town South Florida, Inc., 1655 Palm Beach Lakes Blvd., West Palm Beach, FL 33401, 26-3965524, 8,028,782, 0 Lied Learning and Technology Center for Childhood Deafness and Vision Disorders, 14100 Crawford Street, Boys Town, NE 68010, 47-0841263, 945,997, 0 Nebraska Families Collaborative, 2110 Papillion Parkway, Omaha, NE 68164, 26-4436716, 68,090,179, 48,200.

OtherSecuritiesGrp.Desc: Financial derivatives and other financial products

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: PromiseShip is exempt from federal income taxes under Section 501c3 of the Internal Revenue Code. PromiseShip accounts for uncertainties in accounting for income tax assets and liabilities by recognizing the effect of income tax positions only if those positions are more likely than not of being sustained. At December 31, 2018 and 2017, PromiseShip had no uncertain tax positions accrued.

GrantsOtherAsstToIndivInUSGrp.GrantTypeTxt: Direct cost of youth and families in care

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Funds represent the direct cost of assisting youth in our care, not a disbursement of funds to individuals.
Column b represents the number of items donated.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section A, Line 6
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization is comprised of four members consisting of Child Saving Institute CSI, Father Flanagans Boys Home FFBH, Heartland Family Service HFS, and Omni Behavioral Health Omni.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section A, Line 7a
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The Board of Directors consists of thirteen voting members. The voting members are two employees of and appointed by FFBH one employee of and appointed by CSI one employee of and appointed by HFS one employee of and appointed by OMNI one employee of and appointed by the Nebraska Family Support Network NFSN and seven community members, selected by the Nominating Committee. The Nominating Committee has five members consisting of two employees of FFBH entitled to two votes each, one appointed by CSI with one vote, one appointed by HFS with one vote, and one appointed by OMNI with one vote.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 11b
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: A review was initially performed by an external accounting firm. The treasurer then reviewed the completed Form 990 and provided an electronic copy to all directors for their review prior to filing.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 12c
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: The organization has a conflict of interest policy for the board of directors that is completed by each board member annually. In addition, there is a conflict of interest policy for employees that is signed when the employee is hired, indicating they have read and are aware of the policy.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section B, Line 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Compensation for all positions is determined by using comparable data for similarly situated organizations. Documentation of the decisions made regarding compensation are maintained.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VI, Section C, Line 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Governing documents, conflict of interest policy, and financial statements are made available to the public upon request.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: Form 990, Part VII, Section A, Line 1
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: Judy Rasmussen, Nicholas Juliano, and Dave Reed provide services to Father Flanagans Boys Home and affiliates, therefore remaining weekly hours are spent in that capacity.
Amount consists of payments to foster parents and service providers of 41,334,209, services provided by FFBH 299,472, consulting and training of 257,134, and other professional fees of 91,063.

DisregardedEntityName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Father Flanagan's Fund for Needy Children

BusinessName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: Father Flanagan's Fund for Needy Children
